title: torilis arvensis
intro: Torilis arvensis, also known as wild chervil or hedge parsley, is a biennial or perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Torilis arvensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Torilis arvensis can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
